original_hypothesis.title: Gearboxv3.executeCommands restricts multicall targets to facade, but still allows dangerous facade operations; insufficient command allowlisting may enable unsafe state transitions
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Gearboxv3.executeCommands only checks that each MultiCall target equals the provided facade address, but does not restrict which facade functions may be invoked. If a malicious/compromised facade is configured (or if facade exposes methods that can move funds/permissions in unexpected ways), the keeper can execute arbitrary facade actions that may bypass intended connector-level safety invariants (e.g., enabling/disabling tokens, swapping collateral, changing leverage) without additional validation. While this requires manager authority or misconfiguration, it is operationally relevant given broad strategy execution capabilities.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The connector attempts to constrain call targets but not call semantics. In production, unsafe multicalls are a common source of loss when combined with a permissive strategy engine and external protocol changes.
deployment_assumptions: A facade address is configured/used for Gearbox positions
Keeper/manager can submit arbitrary calls array
Facade contract is upgradable or can change behavior, or is misconfigured to a malicious contract
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment scripts show specific facade addresses or allowlists; executeCommands takes facade as an argument, not stored.
affected_functions: executeCommands(address facade, address creditAccount, MultiCall[] calldata calls, address approvalToken, uint256 amount)

inventory_refs.function: executeCommands(address facade, address creditAccount, MultiCall[] calldata calls, address approvalToken, uint256 amount)

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (calls[i].target != facade) revert; // no further method allowlist besides special-case enableToken tracking

preconditions: OnlyManager access is obtained legitimately or via compromise
Facade permits actions that can move/convert collateral into loss or grant approvals indirectly
Credit account has assets at risk

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Target restriction exists but does not constrain function selectors; approvalToken approval given to creditManager.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time Gearbox credit account is open

operational_questions: What facade functions are intended to be callable via this connector?
Is there an off-chain allowlist of permitted Gearbox multicalls?
Are facade addresses validated as trusted positions in registry before use?
validation_requirements: Enumerate facade ABI and identify high-risk methods that could be called via multicall
Test a malicious facade address scenario in staging (should be rejected operationally?)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Provide a facade address (possibly malicious or misconfigured) and craft calls[] invoking dangerous methods
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Use executeCommands to apply state transitions (token enablement, swaps, debt changes)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Resulting position becomes insolvent or assets transferred/locked

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Unexpected Gearbox account state; liquidation risk; loss of funds
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium to high (depends on ability to unwind credit account)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: executeCommands calls with unusual selectors; facade addresses not matching expected deployments
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Sudden Gearbox account leverage changes without corresponding strategy ticket

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
